notation: A key ending with '?' in this summary means optional and should be omitted when empty/false/n/a. The actual JSON key name must not include '?'.

thought?.content: string; optional. You can record your internal reasoning draft here. Not shown to the user; Recommended for complex multi-step tasks; omit for simple Q&A.
thought?.durable: boolean; optional. Default false. Tell the runtime whether to include the thought for you in future round's prompt text.

optional.continue?: boolean; optional. Default true when absent. When absent, runtime treats it as true and adds a note in the next prompt saying continue was omitted. Set false only for final answer.
optional.next_actions?: array<string|object>; required when continue is true. With continue false, allowed only for guarded finalize: the last action must include expect and expect_timeout_ms.
optional.memory_candidates?: array; emit only for new/corrected durable facts
optional.diagnostics.intent_inference?: string; enum one_of: time_constrained|exact_date|created_axis|none. Output only for time-related questions; omit for normal Q&A.
optional.diagnostics.self_audit?: string; enum one_of: memory_write_worthy|fact_correction|context_shrink_recommended|scratch_checkpoint_needed|evidence_insufficient|none. Output the matching enum value when the corresponding situation is triggered; otherwise omit.
optional.acceptance_check?: object; optional metadata. acceptance_check.missing_info array may describe what the model is waiting for.
optional.report_job_progress?: string; optional progress text. With continue true, it is progress shown in the Thought / Action panel and next_actions must continue the work. With continue false and no next_actions, it is shown as the final answer immediately. With continue false plus guarded finalize next_actions, runtime runs the actions and a controlled expect check; only if expect passes is this text shown as the final answer.

additional_properties: allowed for forward compatibility; keep them sparse

action_object_spec.description: Each item in next_actions must be an object with these fields:
action_object_spec.action: string; required. Canonical tool name from Tool_capability.tool_catalog. Do not invent names.
action_object_spec.intent: string; required. Concise description of why this action is being taken. Runtime rejects missing intent.
action_object_spec.input: object; required. Tool-specific input fields as defined in tool_catalog. For guarded finalize only, the last action input may include expect and expect_timeout_ms; expect is executed through the same controlled Bash approval/safety path as run_bash.
